title: _elementtree.Element: concurrent mutate/read of a shared Element (clear/subelement/tail vs len/tail/iter) corrupts child refcounts with no critical section -> element_dealloc abort (Py_REFCNT==0)
description: Modules/_elementtree.c declares Py_MOD_GIL_NOT_USED but takes NO critical sections on Element operations. A shared Element's `extra` struct (its children array + text/tail) is created/grown/cleared/freed by create_extra / element_add_subelement / element_resize / clear_extra / dealloc_extra / _set_joined_ptr and read by element_length / element_get_tail / element iteration -- all unsynchronized. Concurrent Element.clear() / SubElement() (writers) racing len(el) / el.tail / iter(el) (readers) on one shared Element corrupt the child PyObject* refcounts, so a child (or the Element) is freed while still referenced -> element_dealloc (Modules/_elementtree.c:704) aborts on `assert(Py_REFCNT(op) == 0)` (premature free / double-DECREF). Same root as TSAN-0031 (the TreeBuilder feed race) -- the whole module lacks locking -- but this is the Element-object side and it CRASHES (memory-safety), where TSAN-0031 is the parse-state data race.
sites: Modules/_elementtree.c create_extra / clear_extra / dealloc_extra (the Element `extra` struct: children array + lifecycle, no lock)
Modules/_elementtree.c element_add_subelement / element_resize (grow the children array)
Modules/_elementtree.c element_get_tail / _set_joined_ptr (tail/text read vs write, unlocked)
race_kind: shared mutable object (Element `extra`/children/tail) with no critical section; concurrent mutate (clear/subelement/tail) vs read (len/tail/iter) -> child refcount corruption -> element_dealloc abort / premature free. TSan data-race stanzas AND a crash face.
shared_state: one _elementtree.Element's `extra` struct: its children PyObject* array (and length), and its text/tail pointers, mutated and read concurrently by multiple threads with no synchronization
severity: HIGH (memory-safety). Concurrent access to a shared Element corrupts child refcounts -> premature free; element_dealloc's Py_REFCNT==0 assert catches it. The module opts out of the GIL (Py_MOD_GIL_NOT_USED) but has zero critical sections. Scope caveat: this is concurrent MUTATION of a shared Element; concurrent READ of an immutable shared tree is the common case, but a reader racing one writer already crashes here.
repro_reliability: high: 5/5 crash on debug-ft-nojit and debug-ft-nojit-asan (element_dealloc Py_REFCNT==0 abort), exit-66 TSan data race on debug-ft-nojit-tsan (element_length / _set_joined_ptr / create_extra / clear_extra pairings). NT=8 threads, half clear()+SubElement (writers), half len()+tail read (readers), on one shared Element.
suggested_fix: Take the Element's per-object critical section on the Element mutators and readers (clear/append/subelement/resize/tail-set and len/tail-get/iterate), matching the free-threading hardening the rest of the module needs. Part of the broader _elementtree FT-safety gap (module declares Py_MOD_GIL_NOT_USED with no critical sections); the abandoned PR gh-145569 only covered TreeBuilder.handle_end.
confirmed_build: python_build_matrix/builds/debug-ft-nojit-tsan (CPython 3.16.0a0, --disable-gil --with-thread-sanitizer, heads/main:a1d580430c8). Crash reproduced on debug-ft-nojit and debug-ft-nojit-asan at the same commit. Original fleet build /home/ubuntu/projects/3.16_ft_debug_tsan.
notes: KNOWN — maps to cpython#146022 (the 'make Element FT-safe' umbrella, which describes this exact clear-vs-read crash) + #149816 items 69/87 (fix PR #149918 in flight). NOT a new filing; our repro corroborates #146022. Element-side companion of TSAN-0031 (TreeBuilder). Whole module is Py_MOD_GIL_NOT_USED with zero critical sections (gh-116738 audit). Prior-art searched 2026-07-24 via gh api. Optional: add the 5/5 element_dealloc-abort repro as a corroborating comment on #146022.

related_issues.note: OPEN umbrella 'Make xml.etree.ElementTree.Element usable on free-threaded builds' (@sampsonc). Describes THIS exact crash: '_elementtree.c declares Py_MOD_GIL_NOT_USED but has zero @critical_section; element_getitem checks self->extra!=NULL then derefs children[index] with no lock; concurrent elem.clear() sets self->extra=NULL and frees the children array -> crash.' Our repro is a reproducer for it.

'22 free-threading race conditions' — item (69) 'Unsynchronized extra pointer dereference in len' = our element_length race; item (87) 'Unsynchronized Element.text borrowed-pointer race' = our tail race.
OPEN PR (gh-149816) fixing item 69 (len/extra deref) + attrib race.
TSAN-0031 (our TreeBuilder side) lives in this umbrella; same module-no-locking root.
gh-145568/PR gh-145569 (abandoned) — only added a critical section to TreeBuilder.handle_end.
